content:
Sorry if I missed discussion somewhere, but is it possible to construct Mutex<T> from failable pin init (impl PinInit<T, Error>)? As far as I see, new_mutex!() (and Lock::new in general) accepts only PinInit<T, _ = Infallible>...

Basically I want something like

#[pin_data]
struct S {
    #[pin]
    inner: Mutex<ComplexObject>
}

impl ComplexObject {
      pub fn new() -> impl PinInit<Self, Error>  { /* ... */ }
}

impl S {
    pub fn new() -> impl PinInit<Self, Error> {
        try_pin_init!(Self {
            inner <- new_mutex!(ComlexObject::new())
        })
    }
}
We could add a generic error parameter to Lock::new or add a new variant Lock::try_new that has that generic parameter. But at the moment nobody needed it, so we don't have a way to construct it
Okay, thanks! Then I'll try to implement it for my purposes (and post it here for future references)
Gladyshev Ilya has marked this topic as resolved.
please send a patch so everyone can benefit :)

As far as I have checked, this did the trick:

diff --git a/rust/kernel/sync/lock.rs b/rust/kernel/sync/lock.rs
index 46a57d1fc309..bcef9d39eff3 100644
--- a/rust/kernel/sync/lock.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/sync/lock.rs
@@ -128,12 +139,12 @@ unsafe impl<T: ?Sized + Send, B: Backend> Sync for Lock<T, B> {}

 impl<T, B: Backend> Lock<T, B> {
     /// Constructs a new lock initialiser.
-    pub fn new(
-        t: impl PinInit<T>,
+    pub fn new<E: From<Infallible>>(
+        t: impl PinInit<T, E>,
         name: &'static CStr,
         key: Pin<&'static LockClassKey>,
-    ) -> impl PinInit<Self> {
-        pin_init!(Self {
+    ) -> impl PinInit<Self, E> {
+        try_pin_init!(Self {
             data <- UnsafeCell::pin_init(t),
             _pin: PhantomPinned,
             // SAFETY: `slot` is valid while the closure is called and both `name` and `key` have
@@ -141,7 +152,7 @@ pub fn new(
             state <- Opaque::ffi_init(|slot| unsafe {
                 B::init(slot, name.as_char_ptr(), key.as_ptr())
             }),
-        })
+        } ? E)
     }
 }

I will add this as part of the user's patchset. Alternatively, I can post it as a standalone patch if that is preferable.
yes that should work. But you might need to fix some callsites of Lock::new (which are used by the lock_new! macros)
so in that case it might be better to add a new function Lock::try_new

I added a try_new_mutex! along with a new lock constructor in one of my trees a few weeks back https://github.com/metaspace/linux/blob/6caed7589cab744420e6fc6feea464c662cd232b/rust/kernel/sync/lock.rs#L141
that's not using impl PinInit<T, E> in the arguments, but a plain T
Yes, I think that change did not land for 6.18.
it is in mainline?
I thought so. I think I recall @Daniel Almeida talking about this as well.
yes it's his patch
I just checked again, it is in mainline

Anyway, I had a problem constructing a mutex in a fallible context, because Infallible cannot be coersed to Errorif I recall correctly. This particular piece of code was bugging me:

    KBox::pin_slice(
        |i| {
            let sector = i as u64 * Into::<u64>::into(zone_size_sectors);
            try_new_mutex!(
                if i < zone_nr_conv.try_into().expect("Fewer than 2^32 zones") {
                    ZoneDescriptor {
                        start_sector: sector,
                        size_sectors: zone_size_sectors,
                        capacity_sectors: zone_size_sectors,
                        kind: ZoneType::Conventional,
                        write_pointer: sector + Into::<u64>::into(zone_size_sectors),
                        condition: ZoneCondition::NoWritePointer,
                    }
                } else {
                    ZoneDescriptor {
                        start_sector: sector,
                        size_sectors: zone_size_sectors,
                        capacity_sectors: zone_capacity_sectors,
                        kind: ZoneType::SequentialWriteRequired,
                        write_pointer: sector,
                        condition: ZoneCondition::Empty,
                    }
                }
            )
        },
        zone_count as usize,
        GFP_KERNEL,
    )

Andreas Hindborg said:

Infallible cannot be coersed to Error

we should just fix that :grinning_face_with_smiling_eyes:

impl From<Infallible> for Error {
    fn from(i: Infallible) -> Self {
        match i {}
    }
}
I'll send a patch
we already have that impl
Ah, I see. I thought there was some kind of deeper reason for that :laughter_tears:

Benno Lossin said:

we already have that impl

Wait, it should just work?
hmm that commit is from 2022, so it should've already worked when you tried it...
Let me take another look then.

error[E0277]: the trait bound `Infallible: From<kernel::alloc::AllocError>` is not satisfied
   --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/rnull/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs:31:5
    |
 31 | /     KBox::pin_slice(
 32 | |         |i| {
 33 | |             let sector = i as u64 * Into::<u64>::into(zone_size_sectors);
 34 | |             new_mutex!(
...   |
 57 | |         GFP_KERNEL,
 58 | |     )
    | |_____^ the trait `From<kernel::alloc::AllocError>` is not implemented for `Infallible`
    |
    = help: the trait `From<kernel::alloc::AllocError>` is not implemented for `Infallible`
            but trait `From<!>` is implemented for it
    = help: for that trait implementation, expected `!`, found `kernel::alloc::AllocError`
note: required by a bound in `Box::<T, A>::pin_slice`
   --> /home/aeh/src/linux-rust/rnull/rust/kernel/alloc/kbox.rs:344:12
    |
336 |     pub fn pin_slice<Func, Item, E>(
    |            --------- required by a bound in this associated function
...
344 |         E: From<AllocError>,
    |            ^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^^ required by this bound in `Box::<T, A>::pin_slice`
ah it's the other way around, :thinking:
you have a branch that I can check out?
yes, the one linked above
I'll find the exact place for you
I do not see a branch linked above, only a commit (it doesn't say which branch)
https://github.com/metaspace/linux/blob/6caed7589cab744420e6fc6feea464c662cd232b/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs#L34
ah, it's rnull-v6.18
the commit is fine :)

diff --git a/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs b/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs
index ae6b1ee626e75..641dcf13306d5 100644
--- a/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs
+++ b/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs
@@ -12,7 +12,7 @@
     prelude::*,
     sync::Mutex,
     sync::SpinLock,
-    try_new_mutex,
+    new_mutex,
     types::Owned, //
 };

@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ pub(crate) fn init_zone_descriptors(
     KBox::pin_slice(
         |i| {
             let sector = i as u64 * Into::<u64>::into(zone_size_sectors);
-            try_new_mutex!(
+            new_mutex!(
                 if i < zone_nr_conv.try_into().expect("Fewer than 2^32 zones") {
                     ZoneDescriptor {
                         start_sector: sector,

This patch to trigger the error.
I guess impl From<kernel::alloc::AllocError> for Infallible should just be producing Ok?
it can't, since we're not producing a Result
the issue in this case is that pin_slice requires an error type that's big enough to encompass the AllocError. Infallible means that no error happens, which is incorrect, as the allocation could fail
so it's correct that you get a compile error here.
what we could allow is turning a impl PinInit<T, Infallible> into a impl PinInit<T, E> for any E

We could also change the pin_slice function to this definition:

    pub fn pin_slice<Func, Item, E, E2>(
        mut init: Func,
        len: usize,
        flags: Flags,
    ) -> Result<Pin<Box<[T], A>>, E>
    where
        Func: FnMut(usize) -> Item,
        Item: PinInit<T, E2>,
        E: From<AllocError>,
        E: From<E2>,
But then you'll have to manually specify E due to ambiguity
(better version below)
not sure if that's worth it though

actually here is a better version:

diff --git a/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs b/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs
index ae6b1ee626e7..cb3c1d3332bb 100644
--- a/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs
+++ b/drivers/block/rnull/zoned.rs
@@ -9,10 +9,10 @@
         self,
         gen_disk::GenDiskRef, //
     },
+    new_mutex,
     prelude::*,
     sync::Mutex,
     sync::SpinLock,
-    try_new_mutex,
     types::Owned, //
 };

@@ -31,7 +31,7 @@ pub(crate) fn init_zone_descriptors(
     KBox::pin_slice(
         |i| {
             let sector = i as u64 * Into::<u64>::into(zone_size_sectors);
-            try_new_mutex!(
+            new_mutex!(
                 if i < zone_nr_conv.try_into().expect("Fewer than 2^32 zones") {
                     ZoneDescriptor {
                         start_sector: sector,
diff --git a/rust/kernel/alloc/kbox.rs b/rust/kernel/alloc/kbox.rs
index 622b3529edfc..8dbc58b988f1 100644
--- a/rust/kernel/alloc/kbox.rs
+++ b/rust/kernel/alloc/kbox.rs
@@ -323,7 +323,7 @@ pub fn pin(x: T, flags: Flags) -> Result<Pin<Box<T, A>>, AllocError>
     /// }
     ///
     /// // Allocate a boxed slice of 10 `Example`s.
-    /// let s = KBox::pin_slice(
+    /// let s = KBox::pin_slice::<_, _, Error, _>(
     ///     | _i | Example::new(),
     ///     10,
     ///     GFP_KERNEL
@@ -333,24 +333,31 @@ pub fn pin(x: T, flags: Flags) -> Result<Pin<Box<T, A>>, AllocError>
     /// assert_eq!(s[3].d.lock().a, 20);
     /// # Ok::<(), Error>(())
     /// ```
-    pub fn pin_slice<Func, Item, E>(
+    pub fn pin_slice<Func, Item, E, E2>(
         mut init: Func,
         len: usize,
         flags: Flags,
     ) -> Result<Pin<Box<[T], A>>, E>
     where
         Func: FnMut(usize) -> Item,
-        Item: PinInit<T, E>,
-        E: From<AllocError>,
+        Item: PinInit<T, E2>,
+        AllocError: Into<E>,
+        E2: Into<E>,
     {
-        let mut buffer = super::Vec::<T, A>::with_capacity(len, flags)?;
+        let mut buffer = match super::Vec::<T, A>::with_capacity(len, flags) {
+            Ok(buffer) => buffer,
+            Err(err) => return Err(err.into()),
+        };
         for i in 0..len {
             let ptr = buffer.spare_capacity_mut().as_mut_ptr().cast();
             // SAFETY:
             // - `ptr` is a valid pointer to uninitialized memory.
             // - `ptr` is not used if an error is returned.
             // - `ptr` won't be moved until it is dropped, i.e. it is pinned.
-            unsafe { init(i).__pinned_init(ptr)? };
+            match unsafe { init(i).__pinned_init(ptr) } {
+                Ok(()) => (),
+                Err(err) => return Err(err.into()),
+            }

             // SAFETY:
             // - `i + 1 <= len`, hence we don't exceed the capacity, due to the call to
that doesn't have the inference issue
Makes sense. But do you really need AllocError: Into<E>?
I think this change is worth it. Do you want to send it?

Andreas Hindborg said:

Makes sense. But do you really need AllocError: Into<E>?

yes, the line super::Vec::<T, A>::with_capacity can fail the allocation and return Err(AllocError)

Andreas Hindborg said:

I think this change is worth it. Do you want to send it?

sure

Benno Lossin said:

Andreas Hindborg said:

Makes sense. But do you really need AllocError: Into<E>?

yes, the line super::Vec::<T, A>::with_capacity can fail the allocation and return Err(AllocError)

But AllocError is a named struct, not a generic parameter. The compiler should know this bound already?
I don't understand, E is generic, if we for example fill in Infallible for E that would be incorrect, since AllocError cannot be turned into Infallible
Ah, I got it. Thanks for explaining.
@Benno Lossin did you send this? If not, will you? Or should I send it and add a Co-developed by benno tag?
oh yeah sorry about that, feel free to send it
I think I have to put your SoB. Do you want to see the commit message before I send it?
just send it, I'll reply on list
